lesson_manifest.mission: Greet someone, introduce yourself, and give one short positive or negative statement.
Ask and answer a basic identity exchange with name, city, and place.
Ask what someone wants to eat or drink and answer with one clear preference.
Say a date, weekday, and birthday-style calendar line without reading.
Say where you want to go and combine a larger number with a place or direction line.
Say when something happens, whether you know it, and what language you can or cannot speak.
Ask how someone is, whether they can do something, and restate the line in another time frame.
Meet someone politely, ask age or quantity questions, and answer without freezing.
Use this/that with a classifier and give one short comparison.
Ask for clothing or shoes by color, item type, and quantity.
Order a food or drink item and state one preference between two choices.
Call the waiter, order politely, and ask for the menu or bill.
State a big number, a date, and one have-you-ever-done-it line.
Describe the weather or temperature and ask or answer one travel-direction question.
Run a polite greeting or apology exchange, then add one family or frequency sentence.
Give a short biography, ask one follow-up question, and close with a social formula or toast.
